7	// harnessToSurface maps harness names to their catalog surface identifier.
8	// This is the only place harness→surface is declared; all model resolution
9	// goes through BuiltinCatalog using the surface name.
10	var harnessToSurface = map[string]string{
11		"codex":    "codex",
12		"claude":   "claude",
13		"agent":    "embedded-openai",
14		"opencode": "claude",
15	}
16	
17	// ResolveModelTier returns the concrete model for a given harness and tier
18	// by looking up the tier profile in BuiltinCatalog for the harness's surface.
19	func ResolveModelTier(harness string, tier escalation.ModelTier) string {
20		surface, ok := harnessToSurface[harness]
21		if !ok {
22			return ""
23		}
24		model, _ := BuiltinCatalog.Resolve(string(tier), surface)
25		return model
26	}

28	// BenchmarkArm defines one arm in a benchmark run.
29	type BenchmarkArm struct {
30		Label   string               `json:"label"`
31		Harness string               `json:"harness"`
32		Tier    escalation.ModelTier `json:"tier"`
33		Model   string               `json:"model,omitempty"` // explicit override; empty = resolve from tier
34	}
35	
36	// DefaultBenchmarkArms returns the standard set of arms for a full comparison.
37	func DefaultBenchmarkArms() []BenchmarkArm {
38		return []BenchmarkArm{
39			{Label: "agent-smart", Harness: "agent", Tier: escalation.TierSmart},
40			{Label: "agent-standard", Harness: "agent", Tier: escalation.TierStandard},
41			{Label: "agent-cheap", Harness: "agent", Tier: escalation.TierCheap},
42			{Label: "codex-smart", Harness: "codex", Tier: escalation.TierSmart},
43			{Label: "codex-standard", Harness: "codex", Tier: escalation.TierStandard},
44			{Label: "codex-cheap", Harness: "codex", Tier: escalation.TierCheap},
45			{Label: "claude-smart", Harness: "claude", Tier: escalation.TierSmart},
46			{Label: "claude-standard", Harness: "claude", Tier: escalation.TierStandard},
47			{Label: "claude-cheap", Harness: "claude", Tier: escalation.TierCheap},
48		}
49	}
50	
51	// ResolveArm fills in the model from the tier if not explicitly set.
52	func (a *BenchmarkArm) ResolveArm() {
53		if a.Model == "" {
54			a.Model = ResolveModelTier(a.Harness, a.Tier)
55		}
56	}
57	
58	// BenchmarkArmsToCompare converts a slice of BenchmarkArms into CompareOptions fields.
59	func BenchmarkArmsToCompare(arms []BenchmarkArm, baseOpts RunOptions) CompareOptions {
60		harnesses := make([]string, len(arms))
61		armModels := make(map[int]string, len(arms))
62		armLabels := make(map[int]string, len(arms))
63	
64		for i, arm := range arms {
65			arm.ResolveArm()
66			harnesses[i] = arm.Harness
67			if arm.Model != "" {
68				armModels[i] = arm.Model
69			}
70			armLabels[i] = arm.Label
71		}
72	
73		var opts CompareOptions
74		opts.RunOptions = baseOpts
75		opts.Harnesses = harnesses
76		opts.ArmModels = armModels
77		opts.ArmLabels = armLabels
78		return opts
79	}

18	// TestParseClaudeStream feeds synthetic stream-json events through the
19	// parser and asserts:
20	//
21	//  1. Progress lines (session.start, llm.response, tool.call) are emitted
22	//     for relevant events so the existing TailSessionLogs pipeline can
23	//     render them.
24	//  2. The final aggregated result captures the text, tokens, cost, and
25	//     session id from the authoritative "result" event.
26	//  3. The parser stays lenient in the face of garbage lines.
27	//
28	// The table-driven form exercises multiple event shapes so regressions in
29	// one case don't hide breakage in another.
30	func TestParseClaudeStream(t *testing.T) {
31		cases := []struct {
32			name              string
33			input             string
34			wantTurnCount     int
35			wantToolCalls     int
36			wantInputTokens   int
37			wantOutputTokens  int
38			wantCostUSD       float64
39			wantFinalText     string
40			wantSessionID     string
41			wantModel         string
42			wantProgressTypes []string
43		}{
44			{
45				name: "full stream with tool use and result",
46				input: strings.Join([]string{
47					`{"type":"system","subtype":"init","session_id":"sess-abc","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","tools":["Bash","Read"]}`,
48					`{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"m-1","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Starting"},{"type":"tool_use","id":"tu-1","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"ls"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":120,"output_tokens":42}}}`,
49					`{"type":"user","message":{"content":[{"type":"tool_result","tool_use_id":"tu-1","content":"README.md\nfoo.go"}]}}`,
50					`{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"m-2","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Done."}],"usage":{"input_tokens":260,"output_tokens":88}}}`,
51					`{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"duration_ms":1200,"result":"All done.","usage":{"input_tokens":260,"output_tokens":88},"total_cost_usd":0.0123,"session_id":"sess-abc"}`,
52				}, "\n"),
53				wantTurnCount:     2,
54				wantToolCalls:     1,
55				wantInputTokens:   260,
56				wantOutputTokens:  88,
57				wantCostUSD:       0.0123,
58				wantFinalText:     "All done.",
59				wantSessionID:     "sess-abc",
60				wantModel:         "claude-sonnet-4-6",
61				wantProgressTypes: []string{"session.start", "llm.response", "tool.call", "llm.response"},
62			},
63			{
64				name: "garbage lines are skipped",
65				input: strings.Join([]string{
66					`not json`,
67					`{"type":"system","subtype":"init","session_id":"sess-xyz","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6"}`,
68					`{garbage`,
69					`{"type":"assistant","message":{"content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"tu-2","name":"Read","input":{"path":"/tmp/x"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"output_tokens":5}}}`,
70					`{"type":"result","subtype":"success","result":"ok","usage":{"input_tokens":10,"output_tokens":5},"total_cost_usd":0.001,"session_id":"sess-xyz"}`,
71				}, "\n"),
72				wantTurnCount:     1,
73				wantToolCalls:     1,
74				wantInputTokens:   10,
75				wantOutputTokens:  5,
76				wantCostUSD:       0.001,
77				wantFinalText:     "ok",
78				wantSessionID:     "sess-xyz",
79				wantModel:         "claude-sonnet-4-6",
80				wantProgressTypes: []string{"session.start", "llm.response", "tool.call"},
81			},
82			{
83				name: "text-only assistant without tool_use still emits progress",
84				input: strings.Join([]string{
85					`{"type":"system","subtype":"init","session_id":"sess-t","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6"}`,
86					`{"type":"assistant","message":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"hello"}],"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"output_tokens":2}}}`,
87					`{"type":"result","subtype":"success","result":"hello","usage":{"input_tokens":3,"output_tokens":2},"total_cost_usd":0.0001,"session_id":"sess-t"}`,
88				}, "\n"),
89				wantTurnCount:     1,
90				wantToolCalls:     0,
91				wantInputTokens:   3,
92				wantOutputTokens:  2,
93				wantCostUSD:       0.0001,
94				wantFinalText:     "hello",
95				wantSessionID:     "sess-t",
96				wantModel:         "claude-sonnet-4-6",
97				wantProgressTypes: []string{"session.start", "llm.response"},
98			},
99		}
100	
101		for _, tc := range cases {
102			tc := tc
103			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
104				var progressBuf bytes.Buffer
105				start := time.Now().Add(-3 * time.Second) // exercise elapsed_ms > 0
106				res, err := parseClaudeStream(strings.NewReader(tc.input), &progressBuf, "sess-test", "ddx-12345678", start)
107				require.NoError(t, err)
108				require.NotNil(t, res)
109	
110				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantTurnCount, res.TurnCount, "turn count")
111				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantToolCalls, res.ToolCalls, "tool call count")
112				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantInputTokens, res.InputTokens, "input tokens")
113				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantOutputTokens, res.OutputTokens, "output tokens")
114				assert.InDelta(t, tc.wantCostUSD, res.CostUSD, 1e-9, "cost usd")
115				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantFinalText, res.FinalText, "final text")
116				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantSessionID, res.SessionID, "session id")
117				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantModel, res.Model, "model")
118				assert.False(t, res.IsError)
119	
120				// Walk the emitted progress lines and confirm the expected types
121				// appear in order.
122				var gotTypes []string
123				for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(progressBuf.String()), "\n") {
124					if line == "" {
125						continue
126					}
127					var entry map[string]any
128					require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry), "progress line must be valid JSON: %s", line)
129					t, _ := entry["type"].(string)
130					gotTypes = append(gotTypes, t)
131				}
132				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantProgressTypes, gotTypes, "progress event types (in order)")
133	
134				// Spot-check that at least one tool.call line carries the bead_id
135				// so execute-bead operators can grep the log stream.
136				if tc.wantToolCalls > 0 {
137					assert.Contains(t, progressBuf.String(), `"bead_id":"ddx-12345678"`)
138					assert.Contains(t, progressBuf.String(), `"tool.call"`)
139				}
140			})
141		}
142	}

144	// TestParseClaudeStreamEmpty verifies the parser tolerates an empty stream
145	// (e.g. claude crashed before producing any events) and returns an empty but
146	// non-nil result rather than panicking.
147	func TestParseClaudeStreamEmpty(t *testing.T) {
148		res, err := parseClaudeStream(strings.NewReader(""), nil, "sess-empty", "ddx-0", time.Now())
149		require.NoError(t, err)
150		require.NotNil(t, res)
151		assert.Equal(t, 0, res.TurnCount)
152		assert.Equal(t, 0, res.InputTokens)
153		assert.Empty(t, res.FinalText)
154	}

193	// TestResolveClaudeProgressLogDir documents the precedence rule that fixes
194	// the claude/execute-bead parity gap: the per-run override wins over the
195	// runner-wide config so execute-bead can redirect the JSONL trace into the
196	// execution bundle's embedded/ dir.
197	func TestResolveClaudeProgressLogDir(t *testing.T) {
198		cases := []struct {
199			name string
200			opts RunOptions
201			cfg  Config
202			want string
203		}{
204			{
205				name: "opts override wins over config",
206				opts: RunOptions{SessionLogDir: "/bundle/embedded"},
207				cfg:  Config{SessionLogDir: "/runner/default"},
208				want: "/bundle/embedded",
209			},
210			{
211				name: "falls back to config when opts empty",
212				opts: RunOptions{},
213				cfg:  Config{SessionLogDir: "/runner/default"},
214				want: "/runner/default",
215			},
216			{
217				name: "empty when both unset",
218				opts: RunOptions{},
219				cfg:  Config{},
220				want: "",
221			},
222			{
223				name: "opts override wins even when config empty",
224				opts: RunOptions{SessionLogDir: "/bundle/embedded"},
225				cfg:  Config{},
226				want: "/bundle/embedded",
227			},
228		}
229		for _, tc := range cases {
230			tc := tc
231			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
232				assert.Equal(t, tc.want, resolveClaudeProgressLogDir(tc.opts, tc.cfg))
233			})
234		}
235	}

237	// writeFakeClaudeBinary creates a shell script that mimics the claude CLI's
238	// stream-json output. The script ignores all arguments and prints a minimal
239	// but complete sequence of stream events so runClaudeStreaming has real
240	// bytes to parse and the progress log file ends up with content.
241	func writeFakeClaudeBinary(t *testing.T, dir string) string {
242		t.Helper()
243		path := filepath.Join(dir, "fake-claude")
244		script := `#!/bin/sh
245	cat <<'EOF'
246	{"type":"system","subtype":"init","session_id":"sess-fake","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6"}
247	{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"m-1","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","content":[{"type":"text","text":"hello"}],"usage":{"input_tokens":5,"output_tokens":2}}}
248	{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"duration_ms":10,"result":"hello","usage":{"input_tokens":5,"output_tokens":2},"total_cost_usd":0.0001,"session_id":"sess-fake"}
249	EOF
250	`
251		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(script), 0o755))
252		return path
253	}
254	
255	// findClaudeProgressLog locates the agent-*.jsonl trace file that
256	// runClaudeStreaming writes. Returns the path to the single matching file.
257	func findClaudeProgressLog(t *testing.T, dir string) string {
258		t.Helper()
259		entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
260		require.NoError(t, err)
261		var found []string
262		for _, e := range entries {
263			if e.IsDir() {
264				continue
265			}
266			name := e.Name()
267			if strings.HasPrefix(name, "agent-") && strings.HasSuffix(name, ".jsonl") {
268				found = append(found, filepath.Join(dir, name))
269			}
270		}
271		require.Len(t, found, 1, "expected exactly one agent-*.jsonl in %s, got %v", dir, found)
272		return found[0]
273	}
274	
275	// TestRunClaudeStreaming_OptsSessionLogDirOverridesConfig exercises the
276	// claude streaming path end-to-end and confirms the per-run override
277	// (opts.SessionLogDir) wins over the runner-wide Config.SessionLogDir.
278	// This is the execute-bead parity behavior: the JSONL progress trace must
279	// land in the DDx-owned bundle dir, not the runner's default log dir.
280	func TestRunClaudeStreaming_OptsSessionLogDirOverridesConfig(t *testing.T) {
281		if runtime.GOOS == "windows" {
282			t.Skip("fake claude binary relies on POSIX shell")
283		}
284		tmp := t.TempDir()
285		binPath := writeFakeClaudeBinary(t, tmp)
286		runnerDefaultDir := filepath.Join(tmp, "runner-default")
287		bundleEmbeddedDir := filepath.Join(tmp, "bundle-embedded")
288	
289		r := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: runnerDefaultDir})
290		harness := harnessConfig{Name: "claude", Binary: binPath, PromptMode: "arg"}
291		opts := RunOptions{
292			Harness:       "claude",
293			Prompt:        "hi",
294			SessionLogDir: bundleEmbeddedDir,
295		}
296	
297		result, err := runClaudeStreamingFn(r, context.Background(), harness, "claude", "", opts, "hi", "", 10*time.Second)
298		require.NoError(t, err)
299		require.NotNil(t, result)
300	
301		// Progress trace must land in the opts-provided dir, not the config dir.
302		assert.NoDirExists(t, runnerDefaultDir, "runner default dir must stay empty when opts override is set")
303		logPath := findClaudeProgressLog(t, bundleEmbeddedDir)
304		data, err := os.ReadFile(logPath)
305		require.NoError(t, err)
306		// At minimum the session.start and llm.response events should be present.
307		assert.Contains(t, string(data), `"session.start"`)
308		assert.Contains(t, string(data), `"llm.response"`)
309	}

311	// TestRunClaudeStreaming_FallsBackToConfigLogDir is the regression guard:
312	// when opts.SessionLogDir is empty, the trace must still go to the runner's
313	// configured SessionLogDir (the pre-fix default behavior).
314	func TestRunClaudeStreaming_FallsBackToConfigLogDir(t *testing.T) {
315		if runtime.GOOS == "windows" {
316			t.Skip("fake claude binary relies on POSIX shell")
317		}
318		tmp := t.TempDir()
319		binPath := writeFakeClaudeBinary(t, tmp)
320		runnerDefaultDir := filepath.Join(tmp, "runner-default")
321	
322		r := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: runnerDefaultDir})
323		harness := harnessConfig{Name: "claude", Binary: binPath, PromptMode: "arg"}
324		opts := RunOptions{
325			Harness: "claude",
326			Prompt:  "hi",
327			// SessionLogDir deliberately left empty — must fall back to config.
328		}
329	
330		result, err := runClaudeStreamingFn(r, context.Background(), harness, "claude", "", opts, "hi", "", 10*time.Second)
331		require.NoError(t, err)
332		require.NotNil(t, result)

334		logPath := findClaudeProgressLog(t, runnerDefaultDir)

336		require.NoError(t, err)
337		assert.Contains(t, string(data), `"session.start"`)
338	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"io"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	// Config holds agent service configuration.
11	type Config struct {
12		Profile         string              `yaml:"profile"`          // default routing intent: default, cheap, fast, smart
13		Harness         string              `yaml:"harness"`          // optional forced harness override
14		Model           string              `yaml:"model"`            // optional default model ref or exact pin
15		Models          map[string]string   `yaml:"models"`           // per-harness model overrides
16		ReasoningLevels map[string][]string `yaml:"reasoning_levels"` // per-harness reasoning-level options
17		TimeoutMS       int                 `yaml:"timeout_ms"`       // idle (inactivity) timeout in ms — resets on every stream/event
18		WallClockMS     int                 `yaml:"wall_clock_ms"`    // absolute wall-clock cap in ms — fires regardless of activity
19		SessionLogDir   string              `yaml:"session_log_dir"`  // log directory
20		Permissions     string              `yaml:"permissions"`      // permission level: safe, supervised, unrestricted
21	}
22	
23	// RouteFlags holds raw CLI flag values before normalization into a RouteRequest.
24	// These come directly from parsed command-line arguments.
25	type RouteFlags struct {
26		Profile     string // --profile: default, cheap, fast, smart
27		Model       string // --model: logical ref or exact pin
28		Provider    string // --provider: explicit provider name
29		ModelRef    string // --model-ref: catalog model reference
30		Harness     string // --harness: forced harness override
31		Effort      string // --effort: low, medium, high
32		Permissions string // --permissions: safe, supervised, unrestricted
33	}
34	
35	// RunOptions holds options for a single agent invocation.
36	type RunOptions struct {
37		// Context is the caller's context. When non-nil, the Runner derives its
38		// internal cancel context from this so upstream cancellation (e.g.
39		// server.WorkerManager.Stop) propagates into the provider HTTP call.
40		// Nil defaults to context.Background().
41		Context       context.Context

58	// RunArgs is the SD-024 Stage 2 adapter type that production callers
59	// migrate to in subsequent B22 beads. It collapses the durable knobs
60	// (currently sourced from config.ResolvedConfig in RunWithConfig) and
61	// the per-invocation plumbing (currently in AgentRunRuntime) into a
62	// single flat struct, mirroring RunOptions, so callers don't need to
63	// assemble a ResolvedConfig just to dispatch a single agent run.
64	//
65	// The type is declared here so that B22b/c/d (external-caller
66	// migration beads) and the eventual B22 final removal can reference
67	// it. In this bead it is not yet wired through Run; runInternal still
68	// accepts RunOptions and the public Run signature is preserved.
69	type RunArgs struct {
70		Context       context.Context
71		Harness       string
72		Prompt        string
73		PromptFile    string
74		PromptSource  string
75		Correlation   map[string]string
76		Model         string
77		Provider      string
78		ModelRef      string
79		Effort        string
80		Timeout       time.Duration
81		WallClock     time.Duration
82		WorkDir       string
83		Permissions   string
84		SessionLogDir string
85	}

87	// AgentRunRuntime is the SD-024 successor to RunOptions for the agent
88	// run path. Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
89	// Permissions, Timeout, WallClock, SessionLogDir) are stripped — they
90	// live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's
91	// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
92	// runtime intent remain.
93	//
94	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 2 bead 17.
95	type AgentRunRuntime struct {
96		Prompt                string
97		PromptFile            string
98		PromptSource          string
99		Output                io.Writer
100		Correlation           map[string]string

102		Replay                bool

107		// HarnessOverride, ModelOverride, and PermissionsOverride let a caller

111		// pins harness/model to the reviewer-tier selection.

423	// Default configuration values.
424	const (
425		DefaultHarness = "codex"
426		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
427		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
428		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
429		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
430		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
431		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
432		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
433		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
434		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
435		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
436		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
437		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
438		DefaultLogDir      = ".ddx/agent-logs"
439	)
440	
441	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
442	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
443	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
444	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
445	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
446		if configured == "" {
447			configured = DefaultLogDir
448		}
449		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
450			return configured
451		}
452		if projectRoot == "" {
453			return configured
454		}
455		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
456	}

- Stage 2: RunOptions migration
- Stage 3: ExecuteBeadOptions migration

for the design rationale and 35-bead inventory.

4	// post-merge reviewer. It threads durable knobs from a sealed
5	// config.ResolvedConfig with per-invocation plumbing/intent from an
6	// AgentRunRuntime through the same service-or-runner routing used by
7	// RunWithConfigViaService for `ddx agent run`.
8	
9	import (
10		"context"
11		"fmt"
12	
13		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent"
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"

17	// dispatchViaResolvedConfig is the internal SD-024 dispatch seam shared by
18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
19	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or
20	// a fresh service constructed from projectRoot) and assembles a RunOptions
21	// from rcfg + runtime so every durable knob lands on the dispatched request
22	// exactly once.
23	//
24	// Resolution order:
25	//  1. runner (test injection seam) — used directly via runner.Run.
26	//  2. svc (pre-built service) — used via RunViaServiceWith.
27	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
28	//     and dispatch via RunViaServiceWith.
29	//
30	// Override fields on runtime (HarnessOverride, ModelOverride,
31	// PermissionsOverride, SessionLogDirOverride) take precedence over the
32	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can pin one knob for
33	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
34	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.DdxAgent, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
35		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
36		if harness == "" {
37			harness = rcfg.Harness()
38		}
39		model := runtime.ModelOverride
40		if model == "" {
41			model = rcfg.Model()
42		}
43		permissions := runtime.PermissionsOverride
44		if permissions == "" {
45			permissions = rcfg.Permissions()
46		}
47		sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
48		if sessionLogDir == "" {
49			sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
50		}
51	
52		var opts RunOptions
53		opts.Context = ctx
54		opts.Harness = harness
55		opts.Prompt = runtime.Prompt // evidence:allow-unbounded reason="caller is responsible for bounding the prompt before invoking dispatchViaResolvedConfig; downstream RunViaServiceWith hits readPromptFileBounded for PromptFile inputs"
56		opts.PromptFile = runtime.PromptFile
57		opts.PromptSource = runtime.PromptSource
58		opts.Correlation = runtime.Correlation
59		opts.Model = model
60		opts.Provider = rcfg.Provider()
61		opts.ModelRef = rcfg.ModelRef()
62		opts.Effort = rcfg.Effort()
63		opts.Timeout = rcfg.Timeout()
64		opts.WallClock = rcfg.WallClock()
65		opts.WorkDir = runtime.WorkDir
66		opts.Permissions = permissions
67		opts.SessionLogDir = sessionLogDir
68	
69		if runner != nil {
70			return runner.Run(opts)
71		}
72		if svc == nil {
73			built, err := NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
74			if err != nil {
75				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
76			}
77			svc = built
78		}
79		return RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, projectRoot, opts)
80	}

272	// deprecation warning to stderr if the model pin is deprecated.
273	//
274	// Call this in execute-loop before starting the worker so failures are
275	// surfaced before any beads are claimed rather than mid-execution.
276	func (r *Runner) ValidateForExecuteLoop(harnessName, model, provider, modelRef string) error {
277		if harnessName == "" {
278			return nil // no explicit harness; routing will pick at claim time
279		}
280	
281		h, name, err := r.resolveHarness(RunOptions{Harness: harnessName})
282		if err != nil {
283			return err
284		}
285	
286		if model != "" {
287			cat := r.catalog()
288	
289			// Warn about deprecated model pins before any bead is claimed.
290			if dp, deprecated := cat.CheckDeprecatedPin(model, h.Surface); deprecated {
291				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "execute-loop: model %q is deprecated for harness %q; use %q instead\n",
292					model, name, dp.ReplacedBy)
293			}
294		}
295		return nil
296	}
297	
298	// Capabilities reports the model and reasoning options for a harness.
299	func (r *Runner) Capabilities(name string) (*HarnessCapabilities, error) {
300		harness, harnessName, err := r.resolveHarness(RunOptions{Harness: name})
301		if err != nil {
302			return nil, err
303		}
304	
305		caps := &HarnessCapabilities{
306			Harness:             harnessName,
307			Available:           true,
308			Binary:              harness.Binary,
309			ReasoningLevels:     r.resolveReasoningLevels(harnessName, harness),
310			Surface:             harness.Surface,
311			CostClass:           harness.CostClass,
312			IsLocal:             harness.IsLocal,
313			ExactPinSupport:     harness.ExactPinSupport,
314			SupportsEffort:      harness.EffortFlag != "",
315			SupportsPermissions: len(harness.PermissionArgs) > 0,
316		}
317		if path, err := r.LookPath(harness.Binary); err == nil {
318			caps.Path = path
319		}

# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."

    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
    # debug-go:
    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
    #   glob: "*.go"
    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
    #   run: |
    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     else
    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     fi
    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"

    debug-python:
      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
      glob: "*.py"
      run: |
        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
        else
          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
        fi
      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"

    # Go formatting - native gofmt
    go-fmt:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Format"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        unformatted=$(gofmt -l {staged_files})
        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
          echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
          echo "Run: gofmt -w <files>"
          exit 1
        fi
      skip:
